Why the little mermaid
Jump first. Figure it out on the way down.

Jump first. Figure it out on the way down. She leapt into a world she didn't fully understand and trusted herself to land. That's the energy. Not recklessness. A genuine belief that the leap itself is where the learning happens.

What drains the room

✕ Bureaucracy

Process for process's sake. If the system is slowing the mission, challenge the system.

✕ "It can't be done"

Life has surprised me too many times for this to land. It usually means I haven't tried hard enough.

✕ Death by detail

Know your point. Make it.

✕ Disrespect

Of people's time, intelligence, or effort.

✕ Mediocre

Quality is not negotiable. I care deeply about the standard of everything we put our name on.

Read me correctly

"I can come across as harsh. I mean well."

When I go silent

I am thinking. Learning. Processing. Silence is not absence.

How to unlock the best of me
Deals
Wins
Rooms
More often than not, we win.
1

Engage me

Bring me the full picture. The more context you share, the more connections I can make.

2

Educate me

Share what you know. I am genuinely curious and I learn fast. Don't assume I already know, teach me and I'll run with it.

2

Give me autonomy

I don't thrive under micromanagement. Set the direction, trust me with the path.

3

Give me space to learn

Room to explore, experiment, and occasionally be wrong. Safety to fail fast is part of how I think.

Things that might surprise you
💇

I change my hair a lot

I am just experimenting.

Fun fact

I write romantic tragedy novels.
